name: p38 inhibition restored aged muscle stem-cell regenerative function
summary: Aged mouse muscle stem-cell evidence supports pathway-targeted restoration of self-renewal and regeneration.
statement: In aged mouse muscle stem cells, p38 MAPK pathway inhibition under supportive culture conditions restored self-renewal and improved strength after transplantation into injured aged muscle.
population_or_model: Aged mouse muscle stem cells and injured aged mouse muscle
time_horizon: Ex vivo treatment followed by transplantation and regeneration assays
quantitative_note: The staged baseline treats strength restoration after cell handling/transplantation as animal functional evidence.
caveats: The intervention used ex vivo cell handling and transplantation rather than a simple systemic therapy.
Muscle stem-cell rescue may not generalize to hematopoietic, neural, or epithelial stem-cell compartments.
p38 pathway inhibition has broad biology and safety questions for chronic use.
